fields.definition:
All weapons should have 'primary trigger' and 'secondary trigger' markers as appropriate.
Blurred permutations are called '$primary-blur' and '$secondary-blur'.

fields.name: maximum alternate shots loaded#if the second trigger loads alternate ammunition, this is the maximum number of shots that can be loaded at a time
turn on time#how long after being readied it takes this weapon to switch its 'turned_on' attachment to 1.0
heat recovery threshold:[0,1]#the heat value a weapon must return to before leaving the overheated state, once it has become overheated in the first place
overheated threshold:[0,1]#the heat value over which a weapon first becomes overheated (should be greater than the heat recovery threshold)
heat detonation threshold:[0,1]#the heat value above which the weapon has a chance of exploding each time it is fired
heat detonation fraction:[0,1]#the percent chance (between 0.0 and 1.0) the weapon will explode when fired over the heat detonation threshold
heat loss per second:[0,1]#the amount of heat lost each second when the weapon is not being fired
heat loss#function values sets the current heat loss per second
heat loss venting#function value sets the heat loss per second while weapon is being vented
heat venting exit heat#heat at which to begin the venting exit animations so that the weapon is just about fully cooled when the exit animation completes.
heat illumination:[0,1]#the amount of illumination given off when the weapon is overheated
heat warning threshold#the amount of heat at which a warning will be displayed on the hud
overheated heat loss per second:[0,1]#the amount of heat lost each second when the weapon is not being fired
overheated heat loss#function values sets the heat loss per second when weapon is overheated
clang effect#effect that is played in the air between two players that clang with this weapon
magnification levels#the number of magnification levels this weapon allows
ballistic arcing firing bounds:world units#At the min range (or closer), the minimum ballistic arcing is used, at the max (or farther away), the maximum arcing is used
ballistic arcing fraction bounds:[0-1]#Controls speed and degree of arc. 0 = low, fast, 1 = high, slow
forward movement penalty#percent slowdown to forward movement for units carrying this weapon
sideways movement penalty#percent slowdown to sideways and backward movement for units carrying this weapon
age heat recovery penalty#how much the weapon's heat recovery is penalized as it ages
age rate of fire penalty#how much the weapon's rate of fire is penalized as it ages
age misfire start:[0,1]#the age threshold when the weapon begins to misfire
age misfire chance:[0,1]#at age 1.0, the misfire chance per shot
active camo ding#how much to decrease active camo when a round is fired
handle node#the node that get's attached to the unit's hand
first person weapon offset override#for centered crosshair
support third person camera range:degrees#range in degrees. 0 is straight, -90 is down, 90 is up
melee damage parameters
fields.definition:
damage pyramid angles: defines the frustum from the camera that the melee-attack uses to find targets
damage pyramid depth: how far the melee attack searches for a target

fields.name: damage lunge explosive depth:wu#the distance out from the pyramid center to spawn explosive effects.  This value will be clamped to the damage pyramid depth. 0 defaults to the damage pyramid depth
lunge melee damage#this is only important for the energy sword
lunge melee response#this is only important for the energy sword
empty melee damage#this is only important for the energy sword
empty melee response#this is only important for the energy sword
clang melee damage#this is only important for the energy sword
clang melee response#this is only important for the energy sword
clang melee against melee weapon damage#e.g. used by AR to damage sword guy when clanging sword attack
clang melee against melee weapon damage response#e.g. used by AR to damage sword guy when clanging sword attack
autoaim angle:degrees#the maximum angle that autoaim works at full strength
autoaim range:world units#the maximum distance that autoaim works at full strength
autoaim falloff range:world units#at what point the autoaim starts falling off
autoaim near falloff range:world units#at what point the autoaim reaches full power
magnetism angle:degrees#the maximum angle that magnetism works at full strength
magnetism range:world units#the maximum distance that magnetism works at full strength
magnetism falloff range:world units#at what point magnetism starts falling off
magnetism near falloff range:world units#at what point magnetism reaches full power
deviation angle:degrees#the maximum angle that a projectile is allowed to deviate from the gun barrel due to autoaim OR network lead vector reconstruction
tracking types#specify the kinds of targets this tracking system can lock on
reload dialogue time:seconds#the length of time we wait before saying the reload dialogue
magazine#the magazine from which this trigger draws its ammunition
rounds per shot#the number of rounds expended to create a single firing effect
minimum rounds loaded#the minimum number of rounds necessary to fire the weapon
rounds between tracers#the number of non-tracer rounds fired between tracers
firing noise#how loud this weapon appears to the AI
prediction properties
fields.definition: what the behavior of this barrel is in a predicted network game

fields.name: event_synchronized_projectiles_per_second#Valid only for barrels set to prediction type "continuous". Controls how many projectiles per second can be individually synchronized (use debug_projectiles to diagnose).

maximum_barrel_error_for_event_synchronization#Valid only for barrels set to prediction type "continuous". If the barrel's current error level is over this value (zero to one scale), we will not consider synchronizing projectiles with individual events (use debug_projectiles to diagnose).
firing error
fields.definition:
full error look pitch rate controlls how fast you can turn 
with full error, yaw is implied from pitch. 0==130.
for reference, profile sensitivities are set to:
1: 40
3: 60
9: 130

fields.name: ejection port recovery time#the amount of time (in seconds) it takes for the ejection port to transition from 1.0 (open) to 0.0 (closed) after a shot has been fired
illumination recovery time#the amount of time (in seconds) it takes the illumination function to transition from 1.0 (bright) to 0.0 (dark) after a shot has been fired
heat generated per round:[0,1]#the amount of heat generated each time the barrel fires. Unlike the name suggests, this amount of heat is NOT applied per projectile created.
heat generated per round function&heat generated per round#function value sets the amount of heat to add to the weapon each tick the barrel is firing
age generated per round:[0,1]#the amount the weapon ages each time the trigger is fired
CAMPAIGN age generated per round:[0,1]#the amount the weapon ages each time the trigger is fired
overload time:seconds#the next trigger fires this often while holding down this trigger
runtime rate of fire acceleration rate!
runtime rate of fire deceleration rate!
firing effects#firing effects determine what happens when this trigger is fired
rounds per second#the number of firing effects created per second
rate of fire acceleration#function value sets the current rate of fire when the barrel is firing
acceleration time:seconds#the continuous firing time it takes for the weapon to achieve its final rounds per second
rate of fire deceleration#function value sets the current rate of fire when the barrel is not firing
deceleration time:seconds#the continuous idle time it takes for the weapon to return from its final rounds per second to its initial
barrel spin scale#scale the barrel spin speed by this amount
blurred rate of fire#a percentage between 0 and 1 which controls how soon in its firing animation the weapon blurs
shots per fire#allows designer caps to the shots you can fire from one firing action
fire recovery time:seconds#how long after a set of shots it takes before the barrel can fire again
soft recovery fraction#how much of the recovery allows shots to be queued
melee  fire recovery time:seconds#how long after a set of shots it takes before the weapon can melee
melee soft recovery fraction#how much of the melee recovery allows melee to be queued
deceleration time:seconds#the continuous idle time it takes for the weapon to return to its initial error
damage error#the range of angles (in degrees) that a damaged weapon will skew fire
min error look pitch rate#yaw rate is doubled
full error look pitch rate#yaw rate is doubled
look pitch error power#use to soften or sharpen the rate ding
reload penalty#percentage accuracy lost when reloading
switch penalty#percentage accuracy lost when switching weapons
zoom penalty#percentage accuracy lost when zooming in/out
jump penalty#percentage accuracy lost when jumping
firing penalty function#percentage accuracy lost when the barrel has fired
firing crouched penalty function#percentage accuracy lost when the barrel has fired from a crouched position
moving penalty function#percentage accuracy lost when moving
turning penalty function#percentage accuracy lost when turning the camera
error angle max rotation#angle which represents the maximum input to the turning penalty function.
first person offset:world units#+x is forward, +z is up, +y is left
shot count lower bound#the minimum number of times this firing effect will be used, once it has been chosen
shot count upper bound#the maximum number of times this firing effect will be used, once it has been chosen
firing effect^#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded and fired normally
misfire effect#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded but fired while overheated
empty effect#this effect is used when the weapon is not loaded
optional secondary firing effect#this effect is used when the weapon is not loaded
firing damage#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded and fired normally
misfire damage#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded but fired while overheated
empty damage#this effect is used when the weapon is not loaded
optional secondary firing damage#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded and fired normally
firing rider damage#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded and fired normally
misfire rider damage#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded but fired while overheated
empty rider damage#this effect is used when the weapon is not loaded
optional secondary firing rider damage#this effect is used when the weapon is loaded and fired normally
charging time:seconds#the amount of time it takes for this trigger to become fully charged
charged time:seconds#the amount of time this trigger can be charged before becoming overcharged
cancelled trigger throw#96 was the constant in code for the pp
charged illumination:[0,1]#the amount of illumination given off when the weapon is fully charged
charging effect#the charging effect is created once when the trigger begins to charge
charging damage effect#the charging effect is created once when the trigger begins to charge
charging continuous damage response#plays every tick you're charging or charged, scaled to charging fraction
charged drain rate#how much battery to drain per second when charged
discharge effect#the discharging effect is created once when the trigger releases its charge
discharge damage effect#the discharging effect is created once when the trigger releases its charge
charge fraction^:[0.1]#charging fraction at which the weapon should additionally fire a shot.

options: strict deviation angle#deviation angle is allowed to be less than primary autoaim angle - for the rocket launcher
notifies target units#aiming this weapon at another player can be important to them - for lock-on weapons
non-lunge melee dash disabled#melee-physics dash is disabled on melees that are not lunges
don't drop on dual wield melee

is equipment special weapon#when checked, this weapon
-is deleted when dropped
-does not count against maximum inventory
-gets deleted on host migrations
-prevents swapping or switching weapons
force contrails to come from weapon barrel#setting this forces effects contrails to come from weapon barrel instead of eye point
AI cannot fire tracking projectiles#setting this will remove tracking data from AI-fired projectiles
spew#fires its primary action barrel whenever the trigger is down
latch#fires its primary action barrel when the trigger is down and then latches
latch-autofire#fires its primary action barrel once if pulsed quickly or if not depressed all the way, otherwise does secondary behavior
latch-tether#tethers projectiles if latched long enough, on release the tethered projectile detonates
latch-zoom#latched; fires its primary action barrel when unzoomed, secondary when zoomed
random firing effects#rather than being chosen sequentially, firing effects are picked randomly
can fire with partial ammo#allows a weapon to be fired as long as there is a non-zero amount of ammunition loaded
projectiles use weapon origin#instead of coming out of the magic first person camera origin, the projectiles for this weapon actually come out of the gun
ejects during chamber#this trigger's ejection port is started during the key frame of its chamber animation
projectile vector cannot be adjusted#projectiles fired by this weapon cannot have their direction adjusted by the AI to hit the target
projectiles fire parallel#If there are multiple guns for this trigger, the projectiles emerge in parallel beams (rather than independent aiming)
don't clear fire bit after recovering
prevent marker deviation#the barrel will not fire if all markers are pointed further away from the desired aiming vector than the aim assist deviation angle
error ignores zoom#disables of the barrel error inversely with the zoom magnification
projectile fires in marker direction#projectiles shoot out the marker direction instead of the player's aim vector
silent#ai will not respond to this sound
loud#ai can hear this sound at any range
can fire from partial charge#discharging a partially charged weapon will spew for the charged fraction of the spew time set below
overrides unit and camera screen effects
